title: Authors and Citation
text: Shkolnik D (2026). griddy: Geospatial Distribution Dynamics Simple Features. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/dshkol/griddy.
code: @Manual{,   title = {griddy: Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for Simple Features},   author = {Dmitry Shkolnik},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/dshkol/griddy}, }

title: griddy
text: griddy experimental R package geospatial distribution dynamics sf tidy data. inspired PySAL giddy established spatial distribution dynamics work, especially Rey (2001). methods-novelty project. Prior R implementations exist, including estdaR spdyn. package target maintained, CRAN-grade, long-data, map-ready workflow. Current scope: classify longitudinal spatial values comparable classes estimate classic Markov transition matrices estimate spatial Markov transition matrices conditioned spatial lag class compute simple rank mobility return tidy tables ggplot2 plots API scope still provisional.

title: griddy
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("dshkol/griddy")

title: griddy
text: griddy keeps workflow keyed explicit id, time, value columns instead matrix row position. analytical outputs preserve transition labels, class intervals, spatial-lag intervals can inspected, joined, plotted without reverse-engineering array dimensions.

title: griddy
code: library(griddy) library(dplyr) library(spData) library(spdep) library(tidyr)  data(us_states, package = "spData") data(us_states_df, package = "spData")  states <- us_states |>   left_join(us_states_df, by = c("NAME" = "state")) |>   filter(!NAME %in% c("Alaska", "Hawaii", "Puerto Rico")) |>   arrange(NAME)  panel <- states |>   select(NAME, median_income_10, median_income_15, geometry) |>   pivot_longer(starts_with("median_income_"), names_to = "year", values_to = "income") |>   mutate(year = if_else(year == "median_income_10", 2010L, 2015L))  nb <- poly2nb(states, queen = TRUE)  classes <- classify_dynamics(panel, NAME, year, income, k = 5) classic <- markov_dynamics(classes, NAME, year, class) spatial <- spatial_markov(panel, NAME, year, income, nb = nb, k = 5)  classic$transitions |> select(id, from_time, to_time, transition) |> head() lag_intervals(spatial)

title: Class Intervals Used By A griddy Object — class_intervals
Class Intervals Used By A griddy Object — class_intervals
code: panel <- data.frame(id = rep(1:3, each = 2), year = rep(2020:2021, 3), value = 1:6) classes <- classify_dynamics(panel, id, year, value, k = 3) class_intervals(classes) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   class lower upper type  #>   <ord> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1 Q1     1     2.67 value #> 2 Q2     2.67  4.33 value #> 3 Q3     4.33  6    value

what: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
title: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
text: Converts numeric values long panel ordered classes suitable transition analysis.

title: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
code: classify_dynamics(   data,   id,   time,   value,   method = c("pooled_quantile", "time_quantile", "fixed", "existing"),   k = 5,   breaks = NULL,   labels = NULL )

title: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
text: data data frame sf object long format. id, time, value Columns identifying spatial unit, time, value. method Classification method. "pooled_quantile" uses one set quantile breaks across periods. "time_quantile" uses period-specific ranks. "fixed" uses user-supplied breaks. "existing" treats value already classified state. k Number quantile classes. breaks Breaks method = "fixed". labels Optional class labels.

title: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
text: data frame sf object class column class grd_classes.

title: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(letters[1:4], each = 3),   year = rep(2020:2022, times = 4),   value = c(8, 9, 11, 10, 12, 13, 15, 14, 16, 20, 22, 25) )  classes <- classify_dynamics(panel, id, year, value, k = 3) classes #> <grd_classes> #>    id year value class #> 1   a 2020     8    Q1 #> 2   a 2021     9    Q1 #> 3   a 2022    11    Q1 #> 4   b 2020    10    Q1 #> 5   b 2021    12    Q2 #> 6   b 2022    13    Q2 #> 7   c 2020    15    Q2 #> 8   c 2021    14    Q2 #> 9   c 2022    16    Q3 #> 10  d 2020    20    Q3 #> 11  d 2021    22    Q3 #> 12  d 2022    25    Q3 class_intervals(classes) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   class lower upper type  #>   <ord> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1 Q1      8    11.7 value #> 2 Q2     11.7  15.3 value #> 3 Q3     15.3  25   value

title: griddy: Geospatial Distribution Dynamics — griddy-package

title: Spatial Lag Intervals Used By A Spatial Markov Object — lag_intervals
code: lag_intervals(x)

title: Spatial Lag Intervals Used By A Spatial Markov Object — lag_intervals
Spatial Lag Intervals Used By A Spatial Markov Object — lag_intervals
Spatial Lag Intervals Used By A Spatial Markov Object — lag_intervals
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:4, each = 2),   year = rep(2020:2021, times = 4),   value = c(1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 3, 5, 6) ) nb <- spdep::cell2nb(2, 2) spatial <- spatial_markov(panel, id, year, value, nb = nb, k = 2) lag_intervals(spatial) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   class lower upper type        #>   <ord> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>       #> 1 Q1        3     4 spatial_lag

what: Estimate Classic Markov Transition Dynamics — markov_dynamics
title: Estimate Classic Markov Transition Dynamics — markov_dynamics
text: Estimates transition counts probabilities adjacent periods classified long panel.

title: Estimate Classic Markov Transition Dynamics — markov_dynamics
code: markov_dynamics(classes, id, time, state = class)

title: Estimate Classic Markov Transition Dynamics — markov_dynamics
text: classes classified data frame classify_dynamics() data frame ID, time, state columns. id, time, state Columns identifying spatial unit, time, state.

title: Estimate Classic Markov Transition Dynamics — markov_dynamics
Estimate Classic Markov Transition Dynamics — markov_dynamics
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(letters[1:4], each = 3),   year = rep(2020:2022, times = 4),   value = c(8, 9, 11, 10, 12, 13, 15, 14, 16, 20, 22, 25) )  classes <- classify_dynamics(panel, id, year, value, k = 3) markov <- markov_dynamics(classes, id, year, class)  markov #> <grd_markov> #> 3 states, 8 observed transitions #>      #>             Q1        Q2        Q3 #>   Q1 0.6666667 0.3333333 0.0000000 #>   Q2 0.0000000 0.6666667 0.3333333 #>   Q3 0.0000000 0.0000000 1.0000000 transition_matrix(markov) #>      #>             Q1        Q2        Q3 #>   Q1 0.6666667 0.3333333 0.0000000 #>   Q2 0.0000000 0.6666667 0.3333333 #>   Q3 0.0000000 0.0000000 1.0000000 steady_state(markov) #> Q1 Q2 Q3  #>  0  0  1

title: Plot Rank Mobility — plot_rank_mobility
code: plot_rank_mobility(x)

title: Plot Spatial Markov Matrices — plot_spatial_markov
code: plot_spatial_markov(x)

title: Plot Spatial Markov Matrices — plot_spatial_markov
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:4, each = 2),   year = rep(2020:2021, times = 4),   value = c(1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 3, 5, 6) ) spatial <- spatial_markov(panel, id, year, value, nb = spdep::cell2nb(2, 2), k = 2) plot_spatial_markov(spatial)

title: Plot A Transition Matrix — plot_transition_matrix
code: plot_transition_matrix(x)

title: Plot A Transition Matrix — plot_transition_matrix
code: panel <- data.frame(id = rep(1:4, each = 2), year = rep(2020:2021, 4), value = 1:8) markov <- panel |>   classify_dynamics(id, year, value, k = 2) |>   markov_dynamics(id, year, class)  plot_transition_matrix(markov)

title: Compute Rank Mobility — rank_mobility
text: Computes simple rank changes map-ready exploratory analysis.

title: Compute Rank Mobility — rank_mobility
code: rank_mobility(   data,   id,   time,   value,   compare = c("endpoint", "adjacent"),   descending = TRUE )

title: Compute Rank Mobility — rank_mobility
text: data long data frame sf object. id, time, value Columns identifying spatial unit, time, value. compare "endpoint" compares first last observed periods per unit. "adjacent" compares adjacent periods. descending TRUE, larger values receive better ranks.

title: Compute Rank Mobility — rank_mobility
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(letters[1:4], each = 3),   year = rep(2020:2022, times = 4),   value = c(8, 9, 11, 10, 12, 13, 15, 14, 16, 20, 22, 25) )  rank_mobility(panel, id, year, value) #> <grd_rank_mobility> #> # A tibble: 4 × 10 #>   id     year value  rank start_time end_time start_rank end_rank rank_change #>   <chr> <int> <dbl> <int>      <int>    <int>      <int>    <int>       <int> #> 1 a      2022    11     4       2020     2022          4        4           0 #> 2 b      2022    13     3       2020     2022          3        3           0 #> 3 c      2022    16     2       2020     2022          2        2           0 #> 4 d      2022    25     1       2020     2022          1        1           0 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: abs_rank_change <int>

what: Estimate Spatial Markov Transition Dynamics — spatial_markov
title: Estimate Spatial Markov Transition Dynamics — spatial_markov
text: Estimates transition matrices conditioned class unit's spatial lag start transition period.

title: Estimate Spatial Markov Transition Dynamics — spatial_markov
code: spatial_markov(   data,   id,   time,   value,   nb = NULL,   listw = NULL,   k = 5,   lag_k = k,   class_method = c("pooled_quantile", "time_quantile", "fixed"),   zero.policy = TRUE )

title: Estimate Spatial Markov Transition Dynamics — spatial_markov
text: data long data frame sf object. id, time, value Columns identifying spatial unit, time, value. nb Optional spdep neighbor list. listw Optional spdep weights object. omitted, nb converted spdep::nb2listw(style = "W"). k Number value classes. lag_k Number spatial-lag classes. class_method Value classification method passed classify_dynamics(). zero.policy Passed spdep lag/weight helpers.

title: Estimate Spatial Markov Transition Dynamics — spatial_markov
Estimate Spatial Markov Transition Dynamics — spatial_markov
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(1:4, each = 3),   year = rep(2020:2022, times = 4),   value = c(1, 2, 3, 2, 3, 4, 4, 3, 5, 5, 6, 7) )  nb <- spdep::cell2nb(2, 2) spatial <- spatial_markov(panel, id, year, value, nb = nb, k = 2)  spatial #> <grd_spatial_markov> #> 2 states, 2 lag states, 8 observed transitions #> # A tibble: 8 × 8 #>   lag_class from_state to_state transition     n probability lag_lower lag_upper #>   <ord>     <ord>      <ord>    <chr>      <int>       <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Q1        Q1         Q1       Q1 -> Q1       3       1           3         3.5 #> 2 Q1        Q1         Q2       Q1 -> Q2       0       0           3         3.5 #> 3 Q1        Q2         Q1       Q2 -> Q1       1       0.333       3         3.5 #> 4 Q1        Q2         Q2       Q2 -> Q2       2       0.667       3         3.5 #> 5 Q2        Q1         Q1       Q1 -> Q1       0       0           3.5       5   #> 6 Q2        Q1         Q2       Q1 -> Q2       2       1           3.5       5   #> 7 Q2        Q2         Q1       Q2 -> Q1       0      NA           3.5       5   #> 8 Q2        Q2         Q2       Q2 -> Q2       0      NA           3.5       5   lag_intervals(spatial) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   class lower upper type        #>   <ord> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>       #> 1 Q1      3     3.5 spatial_lag #> 2 Q2      3.5   5   spatial_lag transition_matrix(spatial, "count", lag_class = "Q1") #>    Q1 Q2 #> Q1  3  0 #> Q2  1  2

title: Estimate Stationary Distribution — steady_state
code: steady_state(x)

title: Estimate Stationary Distribution — steady_state
text: x grd_markov object transition probability matrix.

title: Estimate Stationary Distribution — steady_state
code: prob <- matrix(c(0.8, 0.2, 0.3, 0.7), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) steady_state(prob) #> [1] 0.6 0.4

title: Extract A Transition Matrix — transition_matrix
code: transition_matrix(x, type = c("probability", "count"), lag_class = NULL)

title: Extract A Transition Matrix — transition_matrix
text: x grd_markov grd_spatial_markov object. type "probability" "count". lag_class Optional lag class grd_spatial_markov.

title: Extract A Transition Matrix — transition_matrix
code: panel <- data.frame(   id = rep(letters[1:4], each = 3),   year = rep(2020:2022, times = 4),   value = c(8, 9, 11, 10, 12, 13, 15, 14, 16, 20, 22, 25) ) markov <- panel |>   classify_dynamics(id, year, value, k = 3) |>   markov_dynamics(id, year, class)  transition_matrix(markov, "count") #>      #>      Q1 Q2 Q3 #>   Q1  2  1  0 #>   Q2  0  2  1 #>   Q3  0  0  2
